title: elymus laxulus
intro: Elymus laxulus, also known as wild rye, is a type of flowering plant native to North America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties. The health benefits of Elymus laxulus include reducing stress and anxiety, promoting relaxation, and supporting digestive health. Herbal preparations of Elymus laxulus can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, and are often used to soothe nervous system disorders and promote a restful sleep.
